source.language_note: Rulebook in Bahasa Indonesia; numerics reproduced in SI.

full_prompt: Design the primary carbon-fibre main-frame top plate of a KRTI 2025 Racing-Plane (RP) class racing quadcopter. Panduan KRTI 2025 RP class sets MTOW <= 2500 g, airframe is an X-configuration quadrotor with 5-inch (127 mm) propellers, 2207-size brushless motors producing up to 20 N thrust each, 6S LiPo battery. The main-frame top plate is the structural backbone that arm plates bolt to. Geometry: top plate is a flat rectangular CFRP sheet with rounded corners; overall envelope 180 mm (length) x 130 mm (width); thickness 2 mm; 20 mm holes at each corner for arm-bolt access (four arms attach via 4 x M3 bolts each in a 20 mm square pattern); central camera mount is a 40 mm diameter circular relief; flight controller mount is a 30.5 x 30.5 mm square pattern of four M3 through-holes at the plate centroid; battery strap slots are two 80 mm x 8 mm longitudinal slots on the plate centerline spaced 40 mm apart. Material: CFRP woven [0/90]s quasi-isotropic laminate with Ex = Ey = 55 GPa, Gxy = 4 GPa, nu_xy = 0.05, density 1550 kg/m^3, tensile allowable 600 MPa, compressive 550 MPa, ILSS 55 MPa. Load cases: LC1 (Hover reaction) — four motor-arm bolt patterns pulled vertically upward by 20 N each = 80 N total against the plate mass + battery hanging below (~900 g), centered; apply the 80 N vertical as a distributed upward force at each arm pattern, with a 9 N downward gravity force at plate centroid representing battery pack + FC + camera; LC2 (4 g pitch-up maneuver) — 4x LC1 forces: 80 N at each arm, 36 N downward at centroid; LC3 (Crash impact) — 15 g lateral shock with one arm pattern fully fixed (robot landed on one corner); LC4 (Battery retention) — 100 N tension in each of the two battery straps pulling upward through the slots; LC5 (Vibration-surrogate static amplification) — rotor-pass frequency estimate 100 Hz at hover; apply a 3x amplification factor on LC1 loads. Pass/fail: (R1) max stress under LC1, LC2, LC3, LC5 < 400 MPa (composite tensile allowable / 1.5); (R2) under LC1, max vertical deflection at plate centroid < 1.5 mm (maintain camera line-of-sight); (R3) first-mode linear buckling load factor under LC2 >= 3.0 (rotor-arm attachment area); (R4) under LC3, max stress at the fixed corner < 400 MPa and no crack initiation (peak plastic strain proxy < 0.01 in a linear model treating allowable as elastic limit); (R5) under LC4 battery tension, plate material stress < 300 MPa (local bearing); (R6) top plate mass < 35 g so airframe full frame (top + bottom + 4 arms) stays under 220 g total within the 2500 g MTOW budget. Primary verification: linear static structural FEA for all five LCs; linear buckling for LC2. Vibration modal, thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are not explicitly required (vibration is approximated via LC5 amplification on LC1). Output one STEP AP242 single-body plate plus a report listing plate mass, max stress per case, centroid deflection under LC1, buckling factor under LC2.
short_prompt:
Design the primary CFRP main-frame top plate of a KRTI 2025 Racing-Plane (RP) class racing quadcopter. Panduan KRTI 2025 RP class: MTOW <= 2500 g, X-config quadrotor with 5 in (127 mm) props, 2207 motors (up to 20 N thrust each), 6S LiPo.

Envelope: 180 x 130 mm x 2 mm CFRP [0/90]s plate with rounded corners; 20 mm arm-access corner holes each clamping a 20 mm square 4 x M3 arm bolt pattern; 40 mm camera relief at center; 30.5 x 30.5 mm FC mount; two 80 x 8 mm battery-strap slots on centerline at 40 mm spacing.

Factor regime: composite tensile FoS 1.5 (allowable 400 MPa) across all linear cases; linear buckling eigenvalue >= 3.0 at arm attachment under LC2; LC4 local bearing < 300 MPa; LC5 rotor-pass amplification is 3x on the hover loads.

Load cases.
- Hover: 20 N up at each of four arm patterns + 9 N down at the centroid (battery + FC + camera mass).
- 4 g pitch-up: 80 N up at each arm + 36 N down at centroid.
- Crash: 15 g lateral with one arm pattern fully fixed (landed on one corner).
- Battery retention: 100 N tension in each of two straps pulling upward through the slots.
- Vibration surrogate: 3x amplification of the LC1 forces (rotor-pass ~100 Hz).

prompt.title: KRTI 2025 Racing-Plane main frame top plate
prompt.brief: CFRP [0/90]s top plate 180 x 130 x 2 mm for a 5-inch FPV racing quadcopter in KRTI 2025 RP class with 2500 g MTOW.

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Replaced by LC5 amplification surrogate to simplify rulebook-derived eval.
exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Prop aero captured by thrust spec.

out_of_scope_but_real_world_needed: Actual modal analysis at rotor-pass frequencies

eval_coverage: composite flat plate with many cutouts
